title: Level 894
wikitext:
{{Sky Theme}}
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 894: "The Crystal Fields"}} {{Class|p1=3|p2=3|p3=5}}
'''Level 894''' is the 895th level of the Backrooms.
==Overview== {{Blurimage|file=894 rewrite attempt.jpg|caption=A recently harvested area of '''Level 894'''.|hovertext=Fatass Twilights ate all the crystals}} '''Level 894''', nicknamed '''The Crystal Fields''', is estimated to span 1,000,000km2. The grassland that makes up the level is luscious and never seems to grow beyond a certain extent. Omnipresent winds gently blow as the eternal drizzle pours down. Wind turbines are quite common and brand a mint-blue rhombus on both sides of the nacelle. Crystals are commonplace; they can be found littered throughout the fields and quietly hum. The skies are dreary yet pleasing. The sun that radiates the environment seems larger and warmer, while the level's natural satellite appears bluish in hue. The wind turbines found in '''Level 894''' reach 100 meters in height and have a rotor diameter of 180 meters. These wind turbines are in constant operation throughout the year and will reach 40 decibels at their loudest. The turbines would serve as a good shelter due to the crates of supplies found within. Another reason would be their immense durability. They've been theorized to be immune to physical damage. All tests conducted have concluded that this assumption is likely correct. Said tests ranged from basic gunfire and [[Object_12|Liquid Pain]] to attacks from Entity 89, which has been seen effortlessly crushing vehicles and fortifications. Furthermore, the wind turbines can not be tampered with. Such examples would include attempting to change the direction it's facing, shutting a turbine down, and other related things that are not possible. These structures would be more commonly used. However, an estimated 50% of all wind turbines are inaccessible, so their use is limited. ===The Wasteland=== {{Blurimage|file=Decayed wind.jpg|caption=The periphery of '''The Wasteland.}} The Wasteland of '''Level 894''', also known as Decayed Wind, is a hazardous and largely undiscovered subarea found at the center of the level. This zone is estimated to span 2,000km2 and experiences harsh, perpetual sandstorms. Little is known about The Wasteland due to its severe conditions. Wind speeds are more than 65km/h, with the storm's edge reaching 30km/h. The crystals found in The Wasteland are exceptionally large and jagged. Large amounts of crystal fragments are frequently picked up by the intense winds. When this occurs, it is unofficially called a Shardstorm. Shardstorms are extremely dangerous due to the sharp fragments flying through the air at high speeds. This effect is the main reason for this area's lack of information, as even vehicles are useless in the environment. Remote vehicles recovered from The Wasteland often show detrimental damage to the vehicle. Vehicles are commonly peppered with holes. On rarer occasions, electrical damage is present with minor to no visible superficial damage. In the center, a titanic crystal, frequently called The Celestiard, illuminates the sky of the hellscape. Similar to the rest of the area, information about The Celestiard can't be ascertained due to the harsh conditions in the region. Its existence is known due to the minute amount of light that pierces the dust clouds, which allows its silhouette to be partially visible. One of the few things known about The Celestiard would be the phenomena it produces. Only two events are caused by The Celestiard. The Celestiard is known to cause these phenomena as it seems to glow significantly brighter as the events are occurring. These events are relatively scarce, as only four have been documented to have happened since the level's initial discovery. *'''Shardstorm Expansions''': Shardstorm Expansions have only ever been recorded once since '''Level 894's''' initial discovery. As stated before, Shardstorms are dangerous and can prove fatal if one is caught outside. According to the first expansion, signs of an incoming Shardstorm consist of increased rainfall and the shutdown of wind turbines in the affected area two days before the expansion occurred. The storm persisted for approximately 2-4 hours until eventually receding into The Wasteland. Should another expansion happen, it is advised to seek refuge in a wind turbine immediately. If an available turbine can't be located in time, it is recommended to attempt to use one of the many crystal clusters as cover until the storm subsides. *'''Sudden Crystal Growth''': The crystals found naturally in '''Level 894''' are capable of growing rather swiftly. T-S2 typically takes around 8 months to fully mature. The sudden growth of crystals seems to happen every 8-10 years. During this event, the growth time of T-S2 crystals drops significantly. More specifically, its growth time falls to an average of 1 minute. Crystals that have recently burgeoned are 20% brighter and are significantly warmer. This effect may last anywhere from 2 weeks to 2 months. ===Climate=== This level has two documented seasons, spring and winter. Spring temperatures for the plains region are typically a cool 24°C during daylight. Once darkness falls, temperatures fall to 18°C. Spring will always start in the early days of March and persist until late November; winter will start shortly after and commonly ends in the late days of February. The transition between seasons is a sudden process where the temperatures will abruptly rise or lower. This process should take 2-8 minutes. Temperatures during winter sharply plummet, and a light snowfall replaces the constant rain. During winter, it reaches a low of -15°C and plummets further to -25°C at night. The day/night cycle of '''Level 894''' consists of 18 hours of daylight and 20 hours of moonlight during the spring season. On the dawn of the level's winter solstice, sun exposure wanes significantly, leading to a moon-dominated season. The winter day/night cycle comprises 4 hours of daylight and a 30-hour night. Unlike the temperatures during the season's transitional phase, changes in the day cycle are gradual; it takes approximately 1-2 weeks for a complete transition in the day/night cycle. '''Level 894''' has an annual average of 194.19 meters of precipitation, of which 80% (155.33 meters) is rainfall and 20% (38.86 meters) is snowfall. In the spring months, the ever present-thundershower will shift into a thunderstorm once night falls and eventually fades back into a thundershower at sunrise. ==T-S2 Crystals== The clusters commonly found above ground are vastly different from their [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]] counterpart. They naturally grow from the soil of the level. They usually mature in 6-8 months. Matured crystals will consistently measure up to 1.5-2 meters in height and 1-2 meters in width and depth. The crystals, officially designated as T-S2, have a largely unknown composition of minerals, with titanium being the only recognizable element, which makes up approximately 11%. Additionally, T-S2 is an exceptionally durable material. It is twice as durable as steel and has a melting point higher than 2500°C. Due to the impressive strength of T-S2, it would've been used to fabricate tools. However, collection is stagnant due to the sound produced from extracting more than a handful of T-S2. The sounds have been described as a "diabolically loud" resonance. This is caused by the mineral's idiosyncratic qualities. T-S2 is known to amplify sounds to a varying degree. The amplification is dependent on the angle at which the crystal is struck. The effect is usually present in a 1-meter radius of a crystal. When a crystal is hit at a fairly steep angle by a sound wave, it will increase the intensity of the sound. Steeper angles increase the amplification effect, while flatter angles incrementally attenuate sounds. Because of this effect, many extraction operations inadvertently amplify the sounds of the crystal's extraction, causing Entity 89 to investigate the site, usually resulting in numerous casualties for the extraction team. T-S2 crystals possess a form of photoluminescence. However, prior exposure to light isn't required to exhibit this behavior. The amount of light varies from the size of the crystal; smaller shards radiate an amount akin to that of a candle, while a medium-sized chunk is similar to a lantern in light production. Lastly, full-sized crystals emit as much light as the average campfire. T-S2's light-emitting properties prove helpful for low-light conditions if one doesn't have a more effective light source on hand. Moreover, T-S2 emits a mild amount of heat in some cases. This effect commences at nightfall and persists until dawn. ==Entities==
  • Krystalrs
  • Twilights
{{Entity Class|no=89|Dan=5|Hab=Level 894|Int=4|Agg=5}} {{Blurimage|file=Kyr.jpg|caption=A researcher's drawing of a Krystalr.|hovertext=The artist (me) didn't give themselves enough space so they had to partially draw on the other page}} Entity 89, also known as Krystalrs among research teams, are exceedingly dangerous, intelligent crystalline entities that exclusively inhabit '''Level 894'''. Krystalrs have been the bane of wanderers and numerous organizations since the level's discovery because of their aggressive nature towards outsiders. The presence of Krystalrs has effectively exterminated any entities that have found themselves within the level. Unfortunately, humans aren't doing all too well either. Similar to most entities, most wanderers and people alike are killed during their first 1-3 days of residing in the level. People who have been caught by a Krystalr are usually reduced to a red mist from a single strike. To avoid drawing attention, one can try staying relatively quiet, as they rely on sound to pinpoint one's location if at a distance. ==Biology== Krystalrs are colossal, spider-like entities composed of a tougher variant of T-S2. Most Krystalrs attain a stance similar to that of Opiliones. These young adults possess four large, jagged legs, which connect to their jagged central body. On average, they reach 10 meters in height. Regarding the growth cycle of Krystalrs, nothing is known about the adolescent stages of their life cycle. Not a single juvenile has been spotted since the level's discovery. Because of this, and other reasons, it is widely accepted that Krystalrs spend their juvenile years in the array of inaccessible caverns that span the level. This would explain the hyper-territorial behaviors they exhibit and their population surge of approximately every 5 years. As for the young adult stage in their growth cycle, this is the most recurrent of the three age groups. Krystalrs in this stage are often referred to as Knights. This term was chosen because of their territorial behavior and their active nature during these years. Their appearance, as mentioned before, resembles that of Opiliones, more commonly known as harvestmen/daddy longlegs. They possess four large legs, which connect to their smaller, sphere-like central body. Its body hangs approximately 3-4 meters above the ground. Knights typically reach a height of 10 meters, reach speeds of up to 72km/h, and may weigh 40-45 tonnes. Krystalrs above the Knight stage are referred to as Monarchs. Monarchs are usually much grander in form and are the largest Krystalrs can grow. Knights transitioning into this growth phase have broader legs and a marginally elongated central body. Soon after, they will attain a wider stance, and a crown-like growth should be visible atop their body. Some time later, their legs will split in two, giving them eight legs. Fully developed Monarchs may reach heights up to 12-13 meters and are moderately slower than Knights. Concerning durability, Krystalrs are immensely resilient to most forms of damage. Their unwavering strength has proved to be a problem for the [[M.E.G.]]. One test utilized a payload of [[Object 15|Firesalt]] roughly equivalent to 50kg of TNT. This payload was buried while a speaker was hidden close by. Once the investigating Knight was close enough, the payload was detonated. A relatively large piece (3m x 1.5m x 0.5m) from the Knight was heaved hundreds of meters from the blast site. The Knight let out a cry and was staggered from the explosion. A few seconds later, it steadied itself and tunneled away. Now, regarding a Monarch's durability, specifics are currently unknown. However, they are likely to possess greater resilience due to their larger size. ==Behavior== The majority of Knights seem to reside in the cavern system found beneath the surface of the level. Knights residing in the caverns seem to hastily excavate tonnes of rock and soil, which is likely to create more housing space for the growing Krystalr population. Although they typically deviate and tunnel towards any disturbances that are heard, and will emerge in such a way that any organism above it would be pulverized in an instant. After this, they will seal the hole from which they came and patrol the immediate area. The few Knights that are found in the fields will patrol the area where they are located. They will do this for the entire day and typically swap roles with a tunneling Knight at daybreak. At night, Knights above ground will focus their iridescent light, forming a powerful spotlight to aid their vision. This spotlight is capable of light burns if it is focused on a wanderer for too long. Multiple spotlights can be formed, but most Knights seem to prefer a single beam. Monarchs commonly reside within the cavern system and are rarely seen above ground. Monarchs spotted above appear to aimlessly wander the fields and typically don't pursue foreigners for more than a few minutes before losing interest. Their role is claimed to be a caregiver to the adolescent population. While this claim has some merit, no current evidence strongly supports this. Krystalrs communicate with a series of deep, guttural vocalizations. Their cries and growls are most often used to notify nearby Knights of a foreign presence. Krystalrs may emit these sounds to express their distress and frustration. ==Advisories== While following these precautions does not guarantee your survival, it is important that one follows them as they decrease the chances of an encounter. *Staying quiet and lightly walking will reduce the chances of a Knight sensing your presence. *Lay low and slowly crawl if a Knight is within viewing range. *Traveling during night. ===Ill-Advisories=== *Unnecessarily sprinting and producing loud sounds. *Making yourself a visible target to nearby Knights. *Traveling during daylight.
{{Entity Class|no=115|Dan=5|Hab=Level 894, may rarely be found in dark, spacious levels |Int=2|Agg=2}} {{Blurimage|file=Har.png|caption=A researcher's drawing of the average Twilight.|hovertext=Weird creatures}} Entity 115, known as Twilights, are stone giants native to '''Level 894''' but may occasionally be found in poorly lit levels. This entity poses a mild threat to any creature noticed by it. Twilights can somewhat outpace wanderers while hunting, although they are rather sluggish, allowing one to run long enough to cause their interest to fade before it can get up to speed. Despite their slow movement, it is advised to stay clear of any instances to avoid catching the attention of Entity 89 and other Twilights. ==Biology== Twilights are 25 meter tall, headless basalt giants with a large rift in their torso. This rift appears to act as their maw and has a faint golden heat emanating from within. Twilights have long, dense legs and possess 2-4 arms, which vary between raptorial claws that scrape on the ground and spindly arms with sharp, bony hands. If present, its secondary arms wrap around its primary arms' shoulder and will unravel when necessary. Entity 115 is estimated to weigh 80-85 tonnes and can reach a speed of 15km/h if given enough time. Entity 115 has high myopia. Their condition appears to be naturally occurring, as every instance exhibits severe situational unawareness. Because of their nearsightedness, Twilights are attracted to light to aid in locating sustenance and rely on their hearing to locate prey. In terms of durability, Entity 115 bears a resistance to small-arms fire. In most cases, their durability does not matter as Twilights wander in an intangible state. In this state, the entirety of its body, excluding the soles of its feet, is translucent and is capable of traversing through objects. Because of their intangibility, taking shelter in a wind turbine doesn't provide immediate protection, so it is best to wait until Entity 115 loses track of you. The body of Entity 115 has the unique ability to thin the air around itself. To cause this effect, it intakes large amounts of air through the crevices of its body. During this process, the air density will be lowered drastically. After 10 seconds have passed, Entity 115 will expel warm air. This behavior appears to be an automatic function of their bodies, similar to breathing. Another feature of Entity 115 would be its disruption effect on any nearby light sources. More specifically, all forms of natural and artificial light within an approximately 12 meter radius will flicker erratically. This extends to powered-off lights; broken and inactive lights will spontaneously flicker. However, these lights will blink at a slower rate. ==Behavior== Twilights are nocturnal entities that will emerge shortly after the sun has set. They rise from beneath the ground in small numbers, but have appeared in droves before. As stated before, Entity 115 traverses the level while in an intangible state. The entity's diet primarily consists of T-S2, but they are carnivorous as well. While hunting, Entity 115 will pursue illuminated or noisy individuals as long as they are still noticeable. If close enough, the pursuing Twilight will exit its state of intangibility and attempt to grapple its prey. Once it has caught its quarry, it will shove them into its maw. Shortly after this, the individual will be charred and absorbed into the walls of its mouth. As aforementioned, Entity 115 exhibits positive phototaxis behavior. They are most strongly attracted to the light of T-S2. Additionally, they will hone in on moving light sources as well. Twilights seem to know not to attack Entity 89; they will commonly stray away from the area a Krystalr is inhabiting. ==Advisories== *Do not have an active light source on your person. *Travel during the day. *Refrain from making loud noises. ===Ill-Advisories=== *Stand near a light source. *Get within close proximity. *Take shelter in a wind turbine while being chased.
==Colonies & Outposts== ===Thunder Research Station=== The Thunder Research Station is located in a wind turbine that is 2,800 kilometers away from the center of the level. It was established in 2007 and currently 2 [[M.E.G.]] researchers inhabit the station. The station's purpose is to document the weather and any events that may occur in the level. Supplies are airdropped onto the nacelle roof bi-weekly. The station is willing to trade with wanderers and provide information on how to exit. ===Halogen Research Station=== The Halogen Research Station is located on the edge of '''The Wasteland'''.The station was established in 2015 and currently only one M.E.G. researcher inhabits the station. The station's purpose is to monitor changes in The Wasteland's Shardstorm, and document entity behavior and appearance. Supplies airdropped onto the roof every month. The station is not willing to trade or guide wanderers. ==Discovery== '''Level 894''' was reportedly first discovered on October 22nd, 1981 by a John Doe. It was officially discovered on June 15th, 1995 by an exploration team. The team was able to successfully escape with a recording of their experience. Soon after, the M.E.G. sent an expedition team to further document the level. ==Entrances & Exits == ===Entrances=== *T-S2 crystals may appear in other levels; shattering the crystal will take one here. *Wandering through the Turbine Fields of [[Level 231]] long enough will cause it to seamlessly transition to this level. ===Exits=== *On rare occasions, uttering the name Jason may cause one's vision to fade to black. Upon waking up they will find themselves in [[Level 21]]. *Falling asleep or going unconscious in the snow will cause one to awake in [[Level 276]]. Wanderers who have used this exit have reported waking up with a fur coat covering their body. {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information'''|final='''> Close Author & Licensing Information'''|body= '''Author''': [[User:NitrousEnergy|NitrousEnergy]]
